author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
On the words of the Gospel, Matt. xxv. 1, ‘then shall the kingdom of heaven be likened unto ten virgins.’

ccel.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

title: On the words of the Gospel, Matt. xxv. 1, ‘then shall the kingdom of heaven be likened unto ten virgins.’

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ye who were present yesterday remember my promise; which with the Lord’s assistance is to be made good to-day, not to you only, but to the many others also who have come together.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is no easy question, who the ten virgins are, of whom five are wise, and five foolish.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Nevertheless, according to the context of this passage which I have wished should be read again to you to-day, Beloved, I do not think, as far as the Lord vouchsafes to give me understanding, that this parable or similitude relates to those women only who by a peculiar and more excellent sanctity are called Virgins in the Church, whom by a more usual term we are wont also to call, “The Religious;” but if I mistake not this parable relates to the whole Church.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But though we should understand it of those only who are called “the Religious,” are they but ten?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: God forbid that so great a company of virgins should be reduced to so small a number!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But perhaps one may say, “But what if though they be so many in outward profession, yet in truth they are so few, that scarce ten can be found!”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is not so.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For if he had meant that the good virgins only should be understood by the ten, He would not have represented five foolish ones among them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For if this is the number of the virgins which are called, why are the doors of the great house shut against five?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then let us understand, dearly Beloved, that this parable relates to us all, that is, to the whole Church together, not to the Clergy only of whom we spoke yesterday; nor to the laity only; but generally to all.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why then are the Virgins five and five?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: These five and five virgins are all Christian souls together.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But that I may tell you what by the Lord’s inspiration I think, it is not souls of every sort, but such souls as have the Catholic faith, and seem to have good works in the Church of God; and yet even of them, “five are wise, and five are foolish.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: First then let us see why they are called “five,” and why “virgins,” and then let us consider the rest.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Every soul in the body is therefore denoted by the number five, because it makes use of five senses.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For there is nothing of which we have perception by the body, but by the five folded gate, either by the sight, or the hearing, or the smelling, or the tasting, or the touching.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whoso then abstaineth from unlawful seeing, unlawful hearing, unlawful smelling, unlawful tasting, and unlawful touching, by reason of his uncorruptness hath gotten the name of virgin.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But if it be good to abstain from the unlawful excitements of the senses, and on that account every Christian soul has gotten the name of virgin; why are five admitted and five rejected?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They are both virgins, and yet are rejected.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is not enough that they are virgins; and that they have lamps.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They are virgins, by reason of abstinence from unlawful indulgence of the senses; they have lamps, by reason of good works.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Of which good works the Lord saith, “Let your works shine before men, that they may see your good works, and glorify your Father which is in heaven.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Again He saith to His disciples, “Let your loins be girded and your lamps burning.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In the “girded loins” is virginity; in the “burning lamps” good works.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The title of virginity is not usually applied to married persons: yet even in them there is a virginity of faith, which produces wedded chastity.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For that you may know, Holy Brethren, that every one, every soul, as touching the soul, and that uncorruptness of faith by which abstinence from things unlawful is practised, and by which good works are done, is not unsuitably called “a virgin;” the whole Church which consists of virgins, and boys, and married men and married women, is by one name called a Virgin.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whence prove we this?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hear the Apostle saying, not to the religious women only but to the whole Church together; “I have espoused you to One Husband, that I may present you as a chaste virgin to Christ.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And because the devil, the corrupter of this virginity, is to be guarded against, after the Apostle had said, “I have espoused you to one husband, that I may present you as a chaste virgin to Christ;” he subjoined, “But I fear, lest as the serpent beguiled Eve through his subtilty, so your minds should be corrupted from the simplicity that is in Christ.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Few have virginity in the body; in the heart all ought to have it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If then abstinence from what is unlawful be good, whereby it has received the name of virginity, and good works are praiseworthy, which are signified by the lamps; why are five admitted and five rejected?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If there be a virgin, and one who carries lamps, who yet is not admitted; where shall he see himself, who neither preserveth a virginity from things unlawful, and who not wishing to have good works walketh in darkness?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Of these then, my Brethren, yea, of these let us the rather treat.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He who will not see what is evil, he who will not hear what is evil, he that turneth away his smell from the unlawful fumes, and his taste from the unlawful food of the sacrifices, he who refuseth the embrace of another man’s wife, breaketh his bread to the hungry, bringeth the stranger into his house, clotheth the naked, reconcileth the litigious, visiteth the sick, burieth the dead; he surely is a virgin, surely he hath lamps.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What seek we more?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Something yet I seek.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What seekest thou yet, one will say?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Something yet I seek; the Holy Gospel hath set me on the search.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It hath said that even of these, virgins, and carrying lamps, some are wise and some foolish.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: By what do we see this?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: By what make the distinction?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Some great, some exceedingly great thing doth this oil signify.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thinkest thou that it is not charity?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This we say as searching out what it is; we hazard no precipitate judgment.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I will tell you why charity seems to be signified by the oil.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Apostle says, “I show unto you a way above the rest.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Though I speak with the tongues of men and of Angels, and have not charity, I am become as sounding brass, or a tinkling cymbal.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This, that is “charity,” is “that way above the rest,” which is with good reason signified by the oil.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For oil swims above all liquids.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Pour in water, and pour in oil upon it, the oil will swim above.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Pour in oil, pour in water upon it, the oil will swim above.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If you keep the usual order, it will be uppermost; if you change the order, it will be uppermost.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Charity never falleth.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is it then, Brethren?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us treat now of the five wise and the five foolish virgins.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They wished to go to meet the Bridegroom.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is the meaning of “to go and meet the Bridegroom”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: To go with the heart, to be waiting for his coming.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But he tarried.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “While he tarries, they all slept.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is “all”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Both the foolish and the wise, “all slumbered and slept.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Think we is this sleep good?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is this sleep?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Is it that at the tarrying of the Bridegroom, “because iniquity aboundeth, the love of many waxeth cold”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Are we to understand this sleep so?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I like it not.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I will tell you why.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Because among them are the wise virgins; and certainly when the Lord said, “Because iniquity shall abound, the love of many shall wax cold;” He went on to say, “But he that shall endure unto the end, the same shall be saved.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Where would ye have those wise virgins be?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Are they not among those that “shall endure unto the end”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They would not be admitted within at all, Brethren, for any other reason, than because they have “endured unto the end.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: No coldness of love then crept over them, in them love did not wax cold; but preserves its glow even unto the end.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And because it glows even unto the end, therefore are the gates of the Bridegroom opened to them; therefore are they told to enter in, as that excellent servant, “Enter thou into the joy of thy Lord.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What then is the meaning of they “all slept”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: There is another sleep which no one escapes.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Remember ye not the Apostle saying, “But I would not have you to be ignorant, brethren, concerning them which are asleep,” that is, concerning them which are dead?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For why are they called “they which are asleep,” but because they are in their own day?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore “they all slept.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thinkest thou that because one is wise, he has not therefore to die?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Be the virgin foolish, or be she wise, all suffer equally the sleep of death.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But men continually say to themselves, “Lo, the day of judgment is coming now, so many evils are happening, so many tribulations thicken; behold all things which the Prophets have spoken, are well-nigh fulfilled; the day of judgment is already at hand.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They who speak thus, and speak in faith, go out as it were with such thoughts to “meet the Bridegroom.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But, lo! war upon war, tribulation upon tribulation, earthquake upon earthquake, famine upon famine, nation against nation, and still the Bridegroom comes not yet.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whilst then He is expected to come, all they who are saying, “Lo, He is coming, and the Day of Judgment will find us here,” fall asleep.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whilst they are saying this, they fall asleep.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let each one then have an eye to this his sleep, and persevere even unto his sleep in love; let sleep find him so waiting.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For suppose that he has fallen asleep.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Will not He who falls asleep afterwards rise again?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore “they all slept;” both of the wise and the foolish virgins in the parable, it is said, “they all slept.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Lo, at midnight there was a cry made.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is, “at midnight”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When there is no expectation, no belief at all of it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Night is put for ignorance.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: A man makes as it were a calculation with himself: “Lo, so many years have passed since Adam, and the six thousand years are being completed, and then immediately according to the computation of certain expositors, the Day of Judgment will come;” yet these calculations come and pass away, and still the coming of the Bridegroom is delayed, and the virgins who had gone to meet him sleep.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And, lo, when He is not looked for, when men are saying, “The six thousand years were waited for, and, lo, they are gone by, how then shall we know when He will come?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He will come at midnight.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is, “will come at midnight”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Will come when thou art not aware.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why will He come when thou art not aware of it?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hear the Lord Himself, “It is not for you to know the times or the seasons which the Lord hath put in His own power.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “The day of the Lord,” says the Apostle, “will come as a thief in the night.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore watch thou by night that thou be not surprised by the thief.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For the sleep of death—will ye, or nill ye—it will come.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “But when that cry was made at midnight.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What cry was this, but that of which the Apostle says, “In the twinkling of an eye, at the last trump”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “For the trumpet shall sound, and the dead shall be raised incorruptible, and we shall be changed”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And so when the cry was made at midnight, “Behold, the Bridegroom cometh;” what follows?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Then all those virgins arose.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is, “they” all arose?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “The hour will come,” said the Lord Himself, “when all that are in the graves shall hear His voice, and shall come forth.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore at the last trumpet they all arose.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Now those wise virgins had brought oil with them in their vessels; but the foolish brought no oil with them.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is the meaning of “brought no oil with them in their vessels”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is “in their vessels”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In their hearts.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whence the Apostle says, “Our glorying is this, the testimony of our conscience.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: There is the oil, the precious oil; this oil is of the gift of God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Men can put oil into their vessels, but they cannot create the olive.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See, I have oil; but didst thou create the oil?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is of the gift of God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou hast oil.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Carry it with thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is “carry it with thee”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Have it within, there please thou God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For, lo, those “foolish virgins, who brought no oil with them,” wish to please men by that abstinence of theirs whereby they are called virgins, and by their good works, when they seem to carry lamps.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And if they wish to please men, and on that account do all these praiseworthy works, they do not carry oil with them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Do you then carry it with thee, carry it within where God seeth; there carry the testimony of thy conscience.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For he who walks to gain the testimony of another, does not carry oil with him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If thou abstain from things unlawful, and doest good works to be praised of men; there is no oil within.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And so when men begin to leave off their praises, the lamps fail.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Observe then, Beloved, before those virgins slept, it is not said that their lamps were extinguished.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The lamps of the wise virgins burned with an inward oil, with the assurance of a good conscience, with an inner glory, with an inmost charity.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yet the lamps of the foolish virgins burned also.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why burnt they then?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Because there was yet no want of the praises of men.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But after that they arose, that is in the resurrection from the dead, they began to trim their lamps, that is, began to prepare to render unto God an account of their works.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And because there is then no one to praise, every man is wholly employed in his own cause, there is no one then who is not thinking of himself, therefore were there none to sell them oil; so their lamps began to fail, and the foolish betook themselves to the five wise, “give us of your oil, for our lamps are going out.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They sought for what they had been wont to seek for, to shine that is with others’ oil, to walk after others’ praises.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Give us of your oil, for our lamps are going out.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But they say, “Not so, lest there be not enough for us and you, but go ye rather to them that sell, and buy for yourselves.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This was not the answer of those who give advice, but of those who mock.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And why mock they?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Because they were wise, because wisdom was in them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For they were not wise by ought of their own; but that wisdom was in them, of which it is written in a certain book, she shall say to those that despised her, when they have fallen upon the evils which she threatened them; “I will laugh over your destruction.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What wonder then is it, that the wise mock the foolish virgins?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And what is this mocking?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Go ye to them that sell, and buy for yourselves:” ye who never were wont to live well, but because men praised you, who sold you oil.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What means this, “sold you oil”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Sold praises.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Who sell praises, but flatterers?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How much better had it been for you not to have acquiesced in flatterers, and to have carried oil within, and for a good conscience-sake to have done all good works; then might ye say, “The righteous shall correct me in mercy, and reprove me, but the oil of the sinner shall not fatten my head.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Rather, he says, let the righteous correct me, let the righteous reprove me, let the righteous buffet me, let the righteous correct me, than the “oil of the sinner fatten mine head.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is the oil of the sinner, but the blandishments of the flatterer?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Go ye” then “to them that sell,” this have ye been accustomed to do.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But we will not give to you.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Lest there be not enough for us and you.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is, “lest there be not enough”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This was not spoken in any lack of hope, but in a sober and godly humility.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For though the good man have a good conscience; how knows he, how He may judge who is deceived by no one?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He hath a good conscience, no sins conceived in the heart solicit him, yet, though his conscience be good, because of the daily sins of human life, he saith to God, “forgive us our debts;” seeing he hath done what comes next, “as we also forgive our debtors.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He hath broken his bread to the hungry from the heart, from the heart hath clothed the naked; out of that inward oil he hath done good works, and yet in that judgment even his good conscience trembleth.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See then what this, “Give us oil,” is.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They were told “Go ye rather to them that sell.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In that ye have been used to live upon the praises of men, ye do not carry oil with you; but we can give you none; “lest there be not enough for us and you.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For scarcely do we judge of ourselves, how much less can we judge of you?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is “scarcely do we judge of ourselves”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Because, “When the righteous King sitteth on the throne, who will glory that his heart is pure?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It may be thou dost not discover anything in thine own conscience; but He who seeth better, whose Divine glance penetrateth into deeper things, discovereth it may be something, He seeth it may be something, He discovereth something.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How much better mayest thou say to Him, “Enter not into judgment with Thy servant”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yea, how much better, “Forgive us our debts”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Because it shall be also said to thee because of those torches, because of those lamps; “I was hungry, and ye gave Me meat.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What then? did not the foolish virgins do so too?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yea, but they did it not before Him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How then did they do it?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: As the Lord forbiddeth, who said, “Take heed that ye do not your righteousness before men to be seen of them, otherwise ye have no reward of your Father which is in heaven: and when ye pray, be not as the hypocrites, for they love to pray, standing in the streets, that they may be seen of men.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Verily I say unto you, they have received their reward.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They have bought oil, they have given the price; they have bought it, they have not been defrauded of men’s praises, they have sought men’s praises, and have had them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: These praises of men aid them not in the judgment day.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But the other virgins, how have they done?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Let your works shine before men, that they may see your good works, and glorify your Father which is in heaven.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He did not say, “may glorify you.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For thou hast no oil of thine own self.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Boast thyself and say, I have it; but from Him, “for what hast thou that thou hast not received?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then in this way acted the one, and in that the other.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now it is no wonder, that “while they are going to buy,” while they are seeking for persons by whom to be praised, and find none; while they are seeking for persons by whom to be comforted, and find none; that the door is opened, that “the Bridegroom cometh,” and the Bride, the Church, glorified then with Christ, that the several members may be gathered together into their whole.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “And they went in with Him into the marriage, and the door was shut.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Then the foolish virgins came afterwards; but had they bought any oil, or found any from whom they might buy it?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore they found the doors shut; they began to knock, but too late.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is said, and it is true, and no deceiving saying, “Knock, and it shall be opened unto you;” but now when it is the time of mercy, not when it is the time of judgment.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For these times cannot be confounded, since the Church sings to her Lord of “mercy and judgment.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is the time of mercy; repent.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Canst thou repent in the time of judgment?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou wilt be then as those virgins, against whom the door was shut.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Lord, Lord, open to us.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What! did they not repent, that they had brought no oil with them?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yes, but what profiteth them their late repentance, when the true wisdom mocked them?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore “the door was shut.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And what was said to them?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “I know you not.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Did not He know them, who knoweth all things?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What then is, “I know you not?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I refuse, I reject you.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In my art I do not acknowledge you, my art knoweth not vice; now this is a marvellous thing, it doth not know vice, and it judgeth vice.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It doth not know it in the practice of it; it judgeth by reproving it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thus then, “I know you not.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The five wise virgins came, and “went in.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How many are ye, my Brethren, in the profession of Christ’s Name! let there be among you the five wise, but be not five such persons only.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let there be among you the five wise, belonging to this wisdom of the number five.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For the hour will come, and come when we know not.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It will come at midnight, Watch ye.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thus did the Gospel close; “Watch, for ye know neither the day nor the hour.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But if we are all to sleep, how shall we watch?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Watch with the heart, watch with faith, watch with hope, watch with charity, watch with good works; and then, when thou shalt sleep in thy body, the time will come that thou shalt rise.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And when thou shalt have risen, make ready the lamps.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Then shall they go out no more, then shall they be renewed with the inner oil of conscience; then shall that Bridegroom fold thee in His spiritual embrace, then shall He bring thee into His House where thou shall never sleep, where thy lamp can never be extinguished.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But at present we are in labour, and our lamps flicker amid the winds and temptations of this life; but only let our flame burn strongly, that the wind of temptation may increase the fire, rather than put it out.
